reasoning: As of mid-May 2025, reports from March have confirmed that talks between the NFL and ESPN for an equity stake have not only been resurrected but are 'gaining steam' and nearing a completion for a massive asset swap (NFL Media assets for an equity stake in ESPN). This ties in cleanly with ESPN's strategic imperative to heavily bolster their content offerings prior to the launch of their standalone direct-to-consumer (DTC) flagship offering expected in Fall 2025. The lengthy negotiation timeline (having initially started before January 2024, dying off in late 2024, and resurrecting in early 2025) suggests original hurdles have likely been addressed. The Manifold market consensus on May 1 was 0.69, reflecting high optimism drawn from these late March 2025 developments. Maintaining the estimate at 0.69 balances the strong fundamental incentives for both parties against the inherent risks and red tape of finalizing a $2B partnership between media/sports giants by the end of the year.
